title: GetResponse vs Constant Contact (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare GetResponse vs Constant Contact. See pricing, features, and which email marketing tool suits your business best.
h1: GetResponse vs Constant Contact: Detailed Comparison
intro: GetResponse and Constant Contact are both established email marketing platforms, but they serve different business needs. GetResponse excels for course creators and webinar hosts with advanced automation, while Constant Contact focuses on nonprofits and local businesses with straightforward tools. Choose based on your primary use case and required feature depth.
choose_a_if: You want to host webinars and sell online courses with integrated tools
You need a free plan to test the platform before paying
You want advanced automation and funnel-building capabilities
You're willing to invest in a more feature-rich platform
choose_b_if: You're a nonprofit or local business seeking simplicity over features
You need SMS marketing capabilities alongside email
You want to run social media ads from your email platform
You prefer a lower entry price point starting at $12/month

faq.q: Is GetResponse better than Constant Contact?
faq.a: GetResponse offers more comprehensive features for course creators, webinar hosts, and marketers seeking advanced automation and funnel-building. However, Constant Contact is simpler for nonprofits and local businesses, and includes SMS marketing. The better choice depends on your specific business model and technical comfort level.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, GetResponse or Constant Contact?
faq.a: Constant Contact starts at $12/month, making it $7 cheaper than GetResponse's $19/month entry point. However, GetResponse's free tier for up to 500 subscribers provides a cost-free option for testing before committing to paid plans, which Constant Contact doesn't offer.

faq.q: Can I switch from Constant Contact to GetResponse?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms support email list imports, allowing you to migrate your contacts. Most modern email marketing tools can import CSV files or connect via integrations. Plan for some setup time to recreate campaigns and automation workflows in your new platform.
